description: Docker Compose deployment for the self-hosted WinGetty winget repository server.
notes: Requires Docker with Compose support and a running Linux-container-compatible daemon such as Docker Desktop.
Edit $dir\docker-compose.yml before first start if you want to change the defaults.
Edit $dir\caddy-compose.yml and $dir\Caddyfile before first start to configure HTTPS.
Trust the Caddy-issued certificate authority on winget clients, or replace the default config with a publicly trusted certificate.
For local HTTPS on Windows, run as Administrator: Add-Content -Path "$env:WINDIR\System32\drivers\etc\hosts" -Value "`n127.0.0.1 wingetty.local"

Then trust Caddy's root certificate on Windows: docker cp wingetty-caddy:/data/caddy/pki/authorities/local/root.crt "$env:TEMP\wingetty-root.crt"; Import-Certificate -FilePath "$env:TEMP\wingetty-root.crt" -CertStoreLocation Cert:\LocalMachine\Root
Start WinGetty with: docker compose -f "$dir\docker-compose.yml" up -d
Start the HTTPS proxy with: docker compose -f "$dir\caddy-compose.yml" up -d
Stop WinGetty with: docker compose -f "$dir\docker-compose.yml" down
Stop the HTTPS proxy with: docker compose -f "$dir\caddy-compose.yml" down
post_install: Write-Warning 'Docker was not found in PATH. Start WinGetty manually after installing Docker Desktop or another compatible Docker runtime.'
Write-Warning 'Docker is installed, but no running daemon is available. Start Docker Desktop and then start the WinGetty stacks manually.'
Write-Warning 'WinGetty was installed, but the WinGetty stack could not be started automatically. Start it manually once Docker Desktop is running.'
Write-Warning 'WinGetty was installed, but the Caddy HTTPS proxy could not be started automatically. Start it manually once Docker Desktop is running.'
